read: A real TileLang dynamic-slot dKV partial kernel now works when it mirrors the vendored sparse-MQA backward contract: one query token per CTA, caller-owned mutable output, output layout [route_tile, dynamic_slot, D], and T.atomic_addx4 from shared memory. However, the same kernel fails TileLang LayoutInference as soon as token_block > 1, so it cannot yet implement the token-window segmented accumulation needed for compressed-group write reduction.

working_kernel.contract: caller-owned mutable Partials[route_tiles, max_active_groups, D], dynamic PairSlots[token, selected] as the second output dimension, T.atomic_addx4 writeback
working_kernel.why_it_lowers: token_block=1 makes the query token effectively a kernel axis, matching the vendored TileLang sparse-MQA dynamic-index pattern

failed_lowering_checks.failure: TileLang LayoutInference: no available layout found
failed_lowering_checks.lesson: Removing compact TileOffsets is insufficient; dynamic PairSlots in a flat output row remains unsupported.

failed_lowering_checks.failure: TileLang LayoutInference: no available layout found after vector-source staging fix
failed_lowering_checks.lesson: A 3D output layout and T.atomic_addx4 alone are insufficient when the dynamic slot is not tied to a selected-row parallel lane.

failed_lowering_checks.failure: TileLang LayoutInference: no available layout found
failed_lowering_checks.lesson: Making the dynamic slot depend on a selected-row T.Parallel lane is still insufficient when the output is an allocated out_idx tensor.

failed_lowering_checks.failure: TileLang LayoutInference: no available layout found
failed_lowering_checks.lesson: Caller-owned mutable output fixes the per-token pattern, but token-window ownership breaks layout inference.

target_shape_implication.read: The working token_block=1 TileLang kernel has no compressed-group write reduction. It scales like raw selected-row emission and is therefore not competitive with the current 8.0-8.8 ms sparse-atomic wall.

decision: Keep the TileLang harness and the working mutable per-token kernel as a lowering/correctness reference. Do not pursue this exact TileLang path as the speed candidate until TileLang can lower token_window > 1 with dynamic route slots, or until the segmented reducer is rewritten in HIP/CK/rocWMMA where dynamic keyed local accumulation can be controlled directly.
